name: The Bank of Elk River
description_short: The Bank of Elk River is a community bank offering checking, savings, personal loans, business loans up to $8M, mortgages, and digital banking services with access to 37,000+ surcharge-free ATMs.
description_long: The Bank of Elk River is a full-service community bank headquartered in Minnesota (routing number 091903831, phone 763.441.1000) that serves both personal and business customers. The bank positions itself as a relationship-focused institution committed to understanding client goals and providing customized financial solutions at every life stage. The bank offers a comprehensive range of financial products: personal checking and savings accounts, personal loans and lines of credit, home mortgages, business checking and savings accounts, business loans up to $8+ million, treasury management, merchant services, and Autobooks accounting integration. Customers can access services through digital banking platforms, a mobile app (available on App Store and Google Play), and physical branch locations. The bank provides 24/7 access to more than 37,000 surcharge-free ATMs through the MoneyPass network, one of the largest surcharge-free networks in the nation. What distinguishes The Bank of Elk River is its emphasis on personalized service and local decision-making. Marketing materials highlight relationship managers (named examples like "Dusty") who take time to understand business strategy and goals. The bank emphasizes speed and agility in loan processing, with business loan products designed for companies of all types and sizes. The bank also emphasizes community involvement, donating time and financial resources to local causes annually. They offer life planning services through financial advisors to help customers chart courses toward financial goals. As a traditional community bank, The Bank of Elk River likely has higher account minimums and fees compared to online banks, though specific pricing is not detailed on the website. The maximum business loan amount of $8+ million suggests they focus on small-to-mid-sized businesses rather than enterprise clients. While they emphasize personal relationships and local service, this requires in-person or phone interaction rather than the instant digital experiences offered by neobanks.
services: Personal loans and lines of credit
Life planning and financial advisory services
pros: Access to 37,000+ surcharge-free ATMs through MoneyPass network, one of the nation's largest surcharge-free ATM networks
Business loans available up to $8+ million for businesses of all types, sizes, and ages
Mobile app provides full account management including transaction viewing, goal-setting, and budget tools
Offers comprehensive financial planning through trusted financial advisors for life goal planning
Relationship-based approach with named loan officers who understand business strategy and goals
Fast loan processing emphasized in marketing, particularly for business loans with critical timelines
Dual digital banking platforms (personal and business) plus mobile app for 24/7 access
cons: Specific account fees, minimum balances, and interest rates are not disclosed on the website
Physical branch-dependent service model may not appeal to customers seeking fully digital banking experiences
Business loan maximum of $8+ million may be insufficient for mid-market or enterprise businesses
No mention of specialized lending products like SBA loans or equipment financing despite offering business loans
Community bank model typically involves higher fees and lower rates on savings compared to online alternatives

contact.address: 630 Main St Nw, Elk River, MN 55330

best_for: Small-to-mid-sized Minnesota businesses seeking personalized banking relationships and business loans under $8 million
Personal customers in the Elk River/Minnesota area who value face-to-face banking relationships and comprehensive financial planning
Business owners who prioritize speed and agility in loan processing and want advisors who understand their industry and goals
Consumers seeking access to extensive surcharge-free ATM networks without paying ATM fees
diagnosis: The Bank of Elk River is best for small-to-mid-sized business owners and personal customers in Minnesota who value relationship-based banking, personalized service, and comprehensive financial planning. The primary caveat is that as a traditional community bank, they likely charge higher fees and offer lower savings rates than online-only banks, and their service model requires more personal interaction rather than pure digital convenience.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
address: 630 Main St Nw, Elk River, MN 55330
